mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positively driving S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest PAYEMS print showed strong job growth.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past 3%, it signals a high probability of future monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. This increases the discount rate for equities and negatively impacts forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical Fed responses to inflation spikes.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger is active, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a confident consumer with high purchasing power, which directly translates to stronger corporate revenues and positive equity performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is at a multi-decade low of 3.5%.
failure_condition: A model using the interaction of RSAFS momentum and UNRATE level fails to outperform a model using only RSAFS momentum in walk-forward validation (Sharpe ratio).

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of very low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals supply-side constraints and potential margin compression for firms, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent INDPRO data shows a slight contraction.
failure_condition: The negative signal from this interaction is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances during a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.0% indicates a very tight labor market, which drives wage inflation, compresses corporate profit margins, and ultimately leads to lower equity market returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Current unemployment rate is 3.5%, a 50-year low.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when UNRATE is below 4.0% is not statistically different from the average return when UNRATE is above 4.0% (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad economic health, consumer confidence, and fuels activity in related sectors. This positive economic signal supports higher equity prices over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a key leading indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently positive over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an easing of monetary policy, which lowers the cost of capital for firms and reduces the discount rate on future earnings, providing a short-term boost to equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed cut rates three times in the second half of 2019.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a rate cut signal is not statistically significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) growth is the ultimate measure of economic activity. Positive quarter-over-quarter growth directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic health.
failure_condition: Periods of positive quarterly GDPC1 growth are not followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.
